completedPhaseClaims.summary: Created planning and state scaffolding from existing spec/design. No implementation, runtime tests, artifact lint, validation, or certification executed.

Added inline UI Wireframes and User Flows sections to spec.md for connector health, QF packet rendering, evidence bundle export, Telegram summary, action boundary, and schema-mismatch scenarios. No runtime code or certification fields changed.

Cross-repo design reconciliation pass against quantitativeFinance/specs/063-smackerel-companion-bridge/design.md. Added target_context (required) and source_refs (optional) to PersonalEvidenceBundle Bundle Fields table; added analysis_note -> qf/decision-packet+subtype mapping to content-type table; added explicit Reserved Schemas (Not Implemented Pre-MVP) subsection naming QFApprovalAction and QFWatchSignal with pre-MVP handling rules. Added Reconciliation Notes (2026-05-03) section. No spec, scopes, report, uservalidation, runtime source, or certification fields modified.

Plan reconciliation pass (mode: reconcile) folding the 2026-05-03 cross-repo design deltas into scopes.md only. Scope 2 makes the response-level next_cursor canonical (persisted in sync_state.sync_cursor), demotes per-event QFDecisionEvent.cursor to diagnostic-only, and binds QF decision_type to Smackerel content type per the Decision Type To Smackerel Content Type Mapping table (analysis_note preserved as qf/decision-packet + Metadata.decision_subtype). Scope 4 tightens the PersonalEvidenceBundle required field set, marks source_refs optional, and adds field-set parity with QF spec 063 import validation. Scope 5 adds explicit reserved-schema handling per the design's Reserved Schemas (Not Implemented Pre-MVP) subsection (QFApprovalAction normalized as qf/approval-request diagnostic-only with Metadata.reserved=true; QFWatchSignal recorded as diagnostic log only). All scope statuses remain Not Started; no runtime source, spec, design, or uservalidation fields changed; no certification claim made.

Final plan review pass after the prior reconciliation. concerns.resolutionRationale: PARTIAL empirical resolution. The named test `TestQFDecisionsIncompatibleCapabilityBlocksPolling` now exists at tests/e2e/qf_decisions_connector_api_test.go:864 (authored by bubbles.implement Round 5 phase 1, +135 lines appended), compiles cleanly under //go:build e2e, and PASSED in 0.08s against the live disposable test stack (5/5 services Healthy) under `./smackerel.sh --env test test e2e --go-run '^TestQFDecisionsIncompatibleCapabilityBlocksPolling$'` (wrapper exit 0). concerns.sourceDodItem: 4 G028 implementation-reality-scan FAKE_INTEGRATION hits: `internal/connector/qfdecisions/capability.go:109` (`return nil` from validator) and `internal/connector/qfdecisions/normalizer.go:58/91/102` (`return nil, &DegradedDiagnostic{...}` tuple)
concerns.followUpAction: The `implementation-reality-scan.sh` FAKE_INTEGRATION rule fires when a file's path matches `connector|client|adapter` AND the file contains zero external-call patterns AND the file contains suspicious patterns including `return nil`, `noop`, `mock`, `fake`. This produces false positives for pure business-logic helpers inside a connector package (the actual HTTP transport lives in sibling `client.go` and orchestration in `connector.go`). Recommendation: refine the scan to consider package boundary when checking external-call presence, OR exclude `return nil` from suspicious patterns when the function signature returns an error-shaped tuple. Per `.github/copilot-instructions.md` framework-file-immutability policy, this repo does NOT modify `.github/bubbles/scripts/` directly — fix must land upstream in the bubbles repository and be re-installed via `install.sh`.
concerns.rationale: This concern was added by the 2026-05-17 bubbles.harden audit pass after manual triage of all 4 hits confirmed they are pre-existing scan false positives, NOT spec-041 source-code defects. The connector source remains unchanged; no silencing edit was applied to satisfy the scan. The scan should remain a hard governance gate for fabricated integrations; only the rule's path-vs-pattern logic needs refinement to avoid these specific false positives.

The arm must return a valid `qfdecisions.QFCapabilityResponse` envelope containing at minimum `contract_version: 1`, `supported_packet_versions: ["v1"]`, `supported_event_types: ["packet_created"]`, and `audit_envelope_version: 1`. Cross-reference the existing stub patterns at `internal/connector/qfdecisions/connector_test.go:128/225/454/498/542/598/735/878/1109` for the canonical field set; cross-reference the sibling `TestQFDecisionsConnectorIngestsPacketAndRetrievesItThroughSmackerelAPIs` at `qf_decisions_connector_api_test.go:296` which almost certainly already handles this path. After the edit, route to `bubbles.test` to re-run `./smackerel.sh test e2e --go-run '^TestQFDecisionsConnectorIngestsUnknownDecisionTypeWithMetadata$'`. Flip scopes.md line 323 from `[ ]` to `[x]` only after the live-stack run PASSes.
concerns.rationale: Added by the 2026-05-18T13:50:28Z bubbles.test opportunistic narrow-scope unblock run after Round 2R declared the envsubst wrapper-layer blocker resolved and the parent invoked the live-stack runtime proof. The disposable test stack came up Healthy and the envsubst helper auto-installed successfully — both confirming Round 2R worked end-to-end — but the test failed at Connect-time with `qf capability handshake: QF bridge request failed with status 404: 404 Not Found`. Root cause is a test-source omission: the Round 2L author wrote the stub before Round 2N added the Connect-time capability handshake to the connector, and the stub was never updated. The older `C-S2-006-E2E` concern (envsubst root-cause) is now empirically resolved by this run; this new concern names the genuinely residual blocker. Per the iterate-prompt prohibition `Do NOT modify the test source`, the test was NOT edited by `bubbles.test`; routed to `bubbles.implement` for the stub-arm addition. The production unknown-decision-type code path is unchanged and still verified by the unit tests `TestSync_EmitsUnknownDecisionTypeMetricForUnsupportedType` and `TestNormalizerMarksUnknownDecisionTypeWithMetadata` (both PASS in repo HEAD).
concerns.resolvedBy: operator (acting as bubbles.implement) + bubbles.test runtime verification
concerns.resolutionRationale: Operator added the missing `case r.URL.Path == qfdecisions.CapabilitiesPath:` arm at `tests/e2e/qf_decisions_connector_api_test.go:637-654` returning a canonical `qfdecisions.QFBridgeCapability` value (SupportedPacketVersions=[v1], SupportedEventTypes=[packet_created], SupportedDecisionTypes=[recommendation, policy_denial, analysis_note], MaxPageSize=100, MinPageSize=1, SupportedTargetContextTypes=[trip], EvidenceMaxBundleSizeBytes=1048576, EvidenceMaxClaimsPerBundle=50, EvidenceRateLimitPerMinute=60, FreshnessSLAP95Seconds=60, AuditEnvelopeVersion=v1, WatchSignalDirection=qf_to_smackerel, EligibleSmackerelSourceClasses=[watch]). The 2026-05-18T14:04:12Z bubbles.test re-run with the identical -run selector produced `--- PASS: TestQFDecisionsConnectorIngestsUnknownDecisionTypeWithMetadata (0.09s)` with wrapper exit 0 against a live disposable test stack (5/5 services Healthy). DoD scopes.md:320 flipped to `[x]` with evidence anchor to the success-evidence section. The actual canonical capability type in code is `QFBridgeCapability` (not `QFCapabilityResponse` as this concern's followUpAction text referenced); the operator used the correct existing type. Concern marked resolved in place — no `resolvedConcerns[]` array exists in this state.json schema.
